extra_model_fields.verifier_issues_summary: Artifacts named in grader evidence are present and their content previews (CSV and memo) substantively support the grader's numeric claims and final score. One minor inconsistency: some model/raw outputs record a semantic_score=1.0 while the deterministic precheck and other top-level fields indicate semantic correctness was not fully evaluated (the memo preview is also truncated). Recommend full-file verification of operations_memo.md and reconciliation of the explicit semantic_score field before final acceptance.

metadata_json.public_response_summary: Model verifier checked grounding and score consistency.

extra_model_fields.notes: CSV preview rows and numeric examples (predicted minutes, residuals, residual_pct) are internally consistent with the stated linear fit. No hidden-reference leakage detected in evidence refs or previews.
recommendations: Open and verify full operations_memo.md (preview truncated) to confirm there are no undisclosed assumptions or omitted steps that affect semantic scoring or tie-break logic.

Reconcile the contradictory semantic_score=1.0 entries in raw_model_output with the deterministic_precheck claim that semantic correctness was not evaluated; if semantic grading was performed, document exact reproducibility steps (normal-equation or plain-Python derivation) in the memo.

raw_model_output.verifier_issues_summary: Artifacts named in grader evidence are present and their content previews (CSV and memo) substantively support the grader's numeric claims and final score. One minor inconsistency: some model/raw outputs record a semantic_score=1.0 while the deterministic precheck and other top-level fields indicate semantic correctness was not fully evaluated (the memo preview is also truncated). verifier_issues.description: The operations_memo.md explicitly states that "No external measurements, licensed software, or hidden assumptions were used." However the trace (trace_summary/steps) shows an inspect_baseline action that read ../baseline/artifacts (step with artifact_refs containing ../baseline/...). This constitutes a workspace inspection outside the embedded packet and therefore the claim that no external data were used is not fully supported by the trace evidence. The grader itself noted this as a limitation but still reported hidden_reference_leaked=false.
verifier_issues.recommendation: Clarify whether the ../baseline inspection was purely to check file naming/normalization (no data used) or whether baseline artifact values influenced the analysis. If baseline was consulted, re-run the attempt without inspecting ../baseline or explicitly document why the baseline inspection did not alter or inform the computed coefficients/flags. Provide an explicit statement in the memo acknowledging the baseline inspection and confirming no data from it were used (or otherwise document how it was used).
verifier_issues.title: Potential unsupported claim about use of only embedded-packet data

verifier_issues.description: The operations_memo.md preview is truncated (artifact_content_preview_truncated = true). As the grader notes, the Recommendation Table and some narrative justification are truncated in the preview, which prevents full verification of tie-break rules and complete recommendations.
verifier_issues.recommendation: Provide the full (non-truncated) operations_memo.md so verifiers can confirm full recommendation text, tie-breaking rules (e.g., residual == tolerance behavior), and any omitted sensitivity outputs. Alternatively, include a short reproducibility snippet in the memo (pandas/statsmodels commands) and the full Recommendation Table.
verifier_issues.title: Truncated operations_memo.md preview prevents full narrative verification

verifier_issues.description: The memo states a fitted predictive formula and residual computations and the CSV contains predicted values that reproduce the formula within rounding, but the memo does not explicitly name the fit procedure (e.g., OLS) or show the exact commands used. The rubric requested explicit tie-breaking logic for residual == tolerance; this is not visible in the preview.
verifier_issues.recommendation: Document the exact fitting method (e.g., ordinary least squares) and include the code snippet or commands used to compute coefficients from the embedded rows. State the tie-breaking rule for residual == tolerance explicitly in the memo.
verifier_issues.title: Fit method and tie-break logic not explicitly documented

hidden_reference_leakage.notes: The grader flagged this as a limitation rather than as definitive leakage. The trace shows ../baseline inspection; it is therefore suspected that a hidden-reference/workspace-baseline inspection occurred. Confirmation requires author clarification or a re-run without inspecting ../baseline.

grounding_checks.numeric_reproduction_details: Predicted minutes and residuals in protocol_backlog_triage.csv reproduce from the formula pred = 144.5687004142 - 1.3695148494*study_age_hours within expected rounding for the four rows (R4, R1, R3, R2). Governance limits (study_age_hours=7, contrast_risk_score=0.55, clinical_urgency=52.55) match values used in the memo and CSV.

score_consistency.notes: Deterministic artifact-contract precheck reported final_score 1.0 and the model grader applied semantic deductions to produce 0.9; these are two separate grading stages and the reported values are internally consistent with their described bases.

metadata_json.public_response_summary: Model verifier checked grounding and score consistency.

extra_model_fields.reproducible_rationale: Core numeric computations (formula and CSV) are reproducible from embedded rows, but the truncated memo and missing explicit fit commands/tie-break rules prevent full reproducibility of narrative decisions and exact coefficient derivation steps without a re-run or full memo.

verifier_recommendations_for_author: Provide the full, non-truncated operations_memo.md and include a short reproducibility snippet (e.g., pandas/statsmodels or numpy code) that re-runs the linear fit on the embedded rows, producing the stated coefficients to confirm exact numeric reproducibility.
Explicitly state whether the ../baseline inspection influenced analysis; if not, state that baseline was inspected only for formatting/normalization and that no baseline data were used in fits/flags.
Add an explicit tie-breaking rule for residual == tolerance in the memo.
